note: Pre-condition checks applied to each subsample/window before running statistical tests. If a precheck fails, the run verdict is INDETERMINATE with reason=precheck_failed:<name>. Failed runs count in n_indeterminate, not n_decidable.

min_points_per_segment.description: Minimum number of data points in the analysis segment (pre or post window). Below this, statistical tests are unreliable.

min_unique_values_C.description: Minimum number of distinct C values in each window (pre and post). Constant or near-constant C makes Welch/bootstrap degenerate.

max_nan_frac.description: Maximum fraction of NaN values in C or S columns within analysis windows. Above this, data quality is insufficient.

min_variance_C.description: Minimum variance of C in each window. Zero variance makes all parametric tests undefined.

rationale: Standardized preconditions prevent contamination from inapplicable tests (VAR on constant data, Welch on zero-variance windows). Each failure is explicitly labeled, making indetermination diagnosable and reducible.
